content.subject: Task 1: Reshape credentials:* hook surface (types only)
content.description: Update credentials:get/set/delete schemas to (ref, { userId, kind, ... }) shape per Phase 3 plan. Pure-types commit. Build will break at consumers; intentional — Tasks 2-5 fix.

content.subject: Task 2: (userId, ref) storage key shape in credentials-store-db
content.description: Storage key: credential:<userId>:<ref> (was credential:<id>). Update store-blob:put/get input shapes.

content.subject: Task 3: Facade dispatcher + envelope wrap/unwrap
content.description: credentials:get reads encrypted blob, decrypts to envelope, dispatches to credentials:resolve:<kind> if registered. credentials:set wraps payload in envelope before encryption.

content.subject: Task 4: Per-blob mutex serializes concurrent resolves
content.description: Map<userId:ref, Promise> in facade — second caller awaits same promise.

content.subject: Task 5: Update credential-proxy call sites for new shape
content.description: Two call sites in credential-proxy/plugin.ts (lines ~346 in proxy:open-session, ~428 in proxy:rotate-session) update from { id } → { ref, userId } shape.

content.subject: Task 6: Sweep for additional credentials:get consumers
content.description: Grep entire workspace for credentials:get callers beyond the two known sites. Update any found.

content.subject: Task 7: Spike — verify CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN SDK support
content.description: Read vendored SDK source + v1's runner auth section. Decide on Task 9 path (env var vs shim).

content.subject: Task 8: Scaffold @ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth package
content.description: New package with manifest declaring three sub-services. Skeleton init throws not-implemented.

content.subject: Task 9: credentials:resolve:anthropic-oauth (refresh-if-needed)
content.description: 5-min buffer check, POST to Anthropic token endpoint with grant_type=refresh_token, return value + refreshed blob.

content.subject: Task 10: credentials:login + :exchange:anthropic-oauth (PKCE)
content.description: PKCE verifier/challenge generation. Authorize-URL builder. Token-exchange POST.

content.subject: Task 11: Wire proxy:rotate-session into orchestrator
content.description: Investigate runner turn-boundary signal. Subscribe in orchestrator. Gate on OAuth credential presence (api-key sessions stay in coarse mode).

content.subject: Task 12: CLI credentials login anthropic subcommand
content.description: Ephemeral 127.0.0.1 listener + safe-spawn browser opener + state-mismatch CSRF guard. Calls login → exchange → set.

content.subject: Task 13: Wire OAuth plugin into CLI + k8s preset
content.description: Both presets load @ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth. Closes Phase 3 half-wired window.

content.subject: Task 14: dev-agents-stub OAuth-demo agent
content.description: Add anthropic-oauth-demo agent variant for testing rotation path.

content.subject: Task 15: e2e test against real Anthropic token endpoint
content.description: Gated on AX_TEST_ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN. Skips automatically in CI.

content.subject: Task 16: PR notes + boundary review + half-wired closure
content.description: docs/plans/2026-04-29-phase-3-pr-notes.md mirroring Phase 2 format.

content.subject: Task 17: PR open + CI green
content.description: Push branch, open PR, watch CI.

refactor(mcp-client, cli): adopt credentials:get(ref, userId) reshape [Phase 3 I12]

Phase 3 sweep — fold the consumers Phase 1b's memory predicted plus the CLI
command in the same hard-cut commit:

- @ax/mcp-client/src/transports.ts: resolveCredentials() now passes
  { ref, userId: ctx.userId } and treats the result as a string. MCP servers
  run on behalf of the owning agent's user.
- @ax/cli/src/commands/credentials.ts: credentials set <ref> takes a ref
  (not id), wraps the secret as { kind: 'api-key', payload: utf8-bytes,
  userId: 'cli' }. The hardcoded CLI_USER_ID = 'cli' is the single-tenant
  default; Phase 9.5+ replaces with a real auth identity.
- Test stubs in mcp-client (transports, admin-routes) and CLI tests
  (credentials-cli) updated for the new shape.

Build clean across all 47 workspace packages. All test packages green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

SDK confirms — minified code at `sdk.mjs:105` shows:
```
if(!K&&!(Y??process.env).ANTHROPIC_API_KEY&&!(Y??process.env).CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN)
```

The SDK honors `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`. No shim needed. Now grab the v1 OAuth client_id constant:

input.command: ls /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax/src/host/ 2>/dev/null | grep -i credent && echo "---" && grep -rn "client_id\|CLIENT_ID\|oauth" /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax/src/host/credentials.ts 2>/dev/null | head -20

input.description: Read v1 OAuth token exchange + refresh

content.subject: Task 1: Reshape credentials:* hook surface (types only)
content.description: Update credentials:get/set/delete schemas to (ref, { userId, kind, ... }) shape per Phase 3 plan. Pure-types commit. Build will break at consumers; intentional — Tasks 2-5 fix.

content.subject: Task 2: (userId, ref) storage key shape in credentials-store-db
content.description: Storage key: credential:<userId>:<ref> (was credential:<id>). Update store-blob:put/get input shapes.

content.subject: Task 3: Facade dispatcher + envelope wrap/unwrap
content.description: credentials:get reads encrypted blob, decrypts to envelope, dispatches to credentials:resolve:<kind> if registered. credentials:set wraps payload in envelope before encryption.

content.subject: Task 4: Per-blob mutex serializes concurrent resolves
content.description: Map<userId:ref, Promise> in facade — second caller awaits same promise.

content.subject: Task 5: Update credential-proxy call sites for new shape
content.description: Two call sites in credential-proxy/plugin.ts (lines ~346 in proxy:open-session, ~428 in proxy:rotate-session) update from { id } → { ref, userId } shape.

content.subject: Task 6: Sweep for additional credentials:get consumers
content.description: Grep entire workspace for credentials:get callers beyond the two known sites. Update any found.

content.subject: Task 7: Spike — verify CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN SDK support

Spike resolved. Findings to bake into Task 8/9/10:
- `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` is honored by SDK 0.2.119 (sdk.mjs:105) — no shim needed.

- Anthropic token endpoint takes JSON body (not form-urlencoded — my plan was wrong about that).
- Fixed redirect URI `http://localhost:1455/callback` (Anthropic-whitelisted). My plan's "port 0" idea would fail.
- Token exchange request includes `state` parameter (unusual but v1's working code does this).

input.command:
git add packages/credentials-anthropic-oauth tsconfig.json pnpm-lock.yaml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(credentials-anthropic-oauth): scaffold + login/exchange/resolve sub-services [Phase 3]

New plugin registers three sub-services that the @ax/credentials facade
dispatches to when kind === 'anthropic-oauth':

- credentials:resolve:anthropic-oauth — refresh-if-needed (5min buffer, I8).
  Returns cached token when expiresAt is far enough out; refreshes through
  Anthropic's /v1/oauth/token endpoint when within the buffer. Throws
  PluginError(oauth-refresh-failed) on non-2xx — no silent retry (I9).
- credentials:login:anthropic-oauth — build PKCE authorize URL + verifier.
  Verifier and state never log, never persist; CLI command keeps them in
  local memory between :login and :exchange (I13).
- credentials:exchange:anthropic-oauth — POST grant_type=authorization_code,
  return blob + expiresAt + kind. Includes state in exchange body (per v1).

Constants ported from v1 (~/dev/ai/ax/src/host/oauth.ts):
- CLIENT_ID = '9d1c250a-e61b-44d9-88ed-5944d1962f5e' (PKCE public client)
- AUTHORIZE = https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize
- TOKEN     = https://console.anthropic.com/v1/oauth/token
- REDIRECT  = http://localhost:1455/callback (Anthropic-whitelisted)

Task 7 spike confirmed @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk@0.2.119 honors
CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env var (sdk.mjs:105). No SDK shim needed.

Tests: 12 — login URL shape, verifier/state uniqueness, exchange happy
path + error path + missing-input path, resolve cache hit + refresh +
refresh-without-rotation + refresh-failure + malformed blob.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.content:
1	import type { AgentOutcome, Plugin } from '@ax/core';
2	import {
3	  createOrchestrator,
4	  PLUGIN_NAME,
5	  type ChatOrchestratorConfig,
6	  type ChatRunInput,
7	} from './orchestrator.js';
8	
9	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
10	// @ax/chat-orchestrator plugin
11	//
12	// Registers `agent:invoke` — the host-side entrypoint a CLI (or any other host)
13	// uses to drive a single user→agent turn-sequence. Subscribes to `chat:end`
14	// so the orchestrator can capture the outcome the runner emits via its
15	// /event.chat-end IPC POST.
16	//
17	// Everything spicy happens inside `createOrchestrator`; this file is just
18	// the manifest + hook registration.
19	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
20	
21	export function createChatOrchestratorPlugin(
22	  config: ChatOrchestratorConfig,
23	): Plugin {
24	  return {
25	    manifest: {
26	      name: PLUGIN_NAME,
27	      version: '0.0.0',
28	      registers: ['agent:invoke'],
29	      // The orchestrator drives the per-chat lifecycle by calling these
30	      // peers. `session:create` is NOT listed — sandbox:open-session mints
31	      // the session itself; a double-create throws duplicate-session. The
32	      // orchestrator intentionally delegates session minting to the sandbox
33	      // plugin. `ipc:stop` is NOT listed either: sandbox-subprocess stops
34	      // its own per-session listener on child-close (no host-side caller
35	      // needed), and the k8s preset uses @ax/ipc-http which has a
36	      // process-scoped listener with no per-session start/stop hooks.
37	      //
38	      // `agents:resolve` is a HARD dep as of Week 9.5 — every chat goes
39	      // through the ACL gate before the sandbox is spawned. This forces
40	      // any preset that wires the orchestrator to also wire @ax/agents,
41	      // which is the intended coupling.
42	      calls: [
43	        'session:queue-work',
44	        'session:terminate',
45	        'sandbox:open-session',
46	        'agents:resolve',
47	      ],
48	      // ----- conditionally-called peers (NOT in `calls`) -----
49	      //
50	      // Week 10–12 Task 16 (J6) introduces `conversations:get`,
51	      // `conversations:bind-session`, and `session:is-alive`. The
52	      // orchestrator dispatches these ONLY when `ctx.conversationId` is
53	      // set — i.e. on the channel-web path that ALSO loads
54	      // @ax/conversations + a session backend with the new hook. Plugins
55	      // outside that path (CLI canary, mcp-client e2e harness) drive the
56	      // orchestrator without a conversation context, so they don't need
57	      // the peers loaded.
58	      //
59	      // We gate each call with `bus.hasService(...)` at runtime to keep
60	      // the orchestrator usable in those non-channel-web presets. Failing
61	      // closed (no routing, no bind) is the correct degraded behavior —
62	      // a missing conversations plugin can't keep state about active
63	      // sessions anyway.
64	      //
65	      // Same pattern as the existing `ipc:stop` non-declaration: when a
66	      // hook is conditionally consumed, we don't declare it.
67	      // We listen to `chat:end` to capture the outcome emitted by the
68	      // runner (via the IPC server's /event.chat-end handler). The
69	      // subscriber is a pass-through: resolves the waiting deferred,
70	      // returns undefined, doesn't veto and doesn't transform.
71	      //
72	      // We also listen to `chat:turn-end` so that in one-shot mode
73	      // (the default for 6.5a) we queue a `cancel` into the runner's
74	      // inbox after the first user message completes — the runner is
75	      // persistent by design, so without this signal it would block
76	      // forever on inbox.next() and the agent:invoke would time out.
77	      subscribes: ['chat:end', 'chat:turn-end'],
78	    },
79	    init({ bus }) {
80	      const orch = createOrchestrator(bus, config);
81	
82	      bus.registerService<ChatRunInput, AgentOutcome>(
83	        'agent:invoke',
84	        PLUGIN_NAME,
85	        async (ctx, input) => orch.runChat(ctx, input),
86	      );
87	
88	      bus.subscribe<{ outcome: AgentOutcome }>(
89	        'chat:end',
90	        PLUGIN_NAME,
91	        async (ctx, payload) => {
92	          orch.onChatEnd(ctx, payload);
93	          return undefined; // pass-through; no transform, no veto.
94	        },
95	      );
96	
97	      bus.subscribe<{ reason?: string }>(
98	        'chat:turn-end',
99	        PLUGIN_NAME,
100	        async (ctx) => {
101	          orch.onTurnEnd(ctx);
102	          return undefined;
103	        },
104	      );
105	    },
106	  };
107	}
108

content.subject: Task 1: Reshape credentials:* hook surface (types only)
content.description: Update credentials:get/set/delete schemas to (ref, { userId, kind, ... }) shape per Phase 3 plan. Pure-types commit. Build will break at consumers; intentional — Tasks 2-5 fix.

content.subject: Task 2: (userId, ref) storage key shape in credentials-store-db
content.description: Storage key: credential:<userId>:<ref> (was credential:<id>). Update store-blob:put/get input shapes.

content.subject: Task 3: Facade dispatcher + envelope wrap/unwrap
content.description: credentials:get reads encrypted blob, decrypts to envelope, dispatches to credentials:resolve:<kind> if registered. credentials:set wraps payload in envelope before encryption.

content.subject: Task 4: Per-blob mutex serializes concurrent resolves
content.description: Map<userId:ref, Promise> in facade — second caller awaits same promise.

content.subject: Task 5: Update credential-proxy call sites for new shape
content.description: Two call sites in credential-proxy/plugin.ts (lines ~346 in proxy:open-session, ~428 in proxy:rotate-session) update from { id } → { ref, userId } shape.

content.subject: Task 6: Sweep for additional credentials:get consumers
content.description: Grep entire workspace for credentials:get callers beyond the two known sites. Update any found.

content.subject: Task 7: Spike — verify CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN SDK support
content.description: Read vendored SDK source + v1's runner auth section. Decide on Task 9 path (env var vs shim).

content.subject: Task 8: Scaffold @ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth package
content.description: New package with manifest declaring three sub-services. Skeleton init throws not-implemented.

content.subject: Task 9: credentials:resolve:anthropic-oauth (refresh-if-needed)
content.description: 5-min buffer check, POST to Anthropic token endpoint with grant_type=refresh_token, return value + refreshed blob.

content.subject: Task 10: credentials:login + :exchange:anthropic-oauth (PKCE)
content.description: PKCE verifier/challenge generation. Authorize-URL builder. Token-exchange POST.

content.subject: Task 11: Wire proxy:rotate-session into orchestrator
content.description: Investigate runner turn-boundary signal. Subscribe in orchestrator. Gate on OAuth credential presence (api-key sessions stay in coarse mode).

content.subject: Task 12: CLI credentials login anthropic subcommand
content.description: Ephemeral 127.0.0.1 listener + safe-spawn browser opener + state-mismatch CSRF guard. Calls login → exchange → set.

content.subject: Task 13: Wire OAuth plugin into CLI + k8s preset
content.description: Both presets load @ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth. Closes Phase 3 half-wired window.

content.subject: Task 14: dev-agents-stub OAuth-demo agent
content.description: Add anthropic-oauth-demo agent variant for testing rotation path.

content.subject: Task 15: e2e test against real Anthropic token endpoint
content.description: Gated on AX_TEST_ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN. Skips automatically in CI.

content.subject: Task 16: PR notes + boundary review + half-wired closure
content.description: docs/plans/2026-04-29-phase-3-pr-notes.md mirroring Phase 2 format.

content.subject: Task 17: PR open + CI green
content.description: Push branch, open PR, watch CI.

lines: +    // I10 — rotate proxy credentials BEFORE the one-shot cancel, so that any
+    // tool-call follow-ups inside the same turn (model→tool→model) pick up
+    // the refreshed token. api-key-only sessions skip rotation: their kind
+    // never refreshes, and Phase 2's coarse mode is the desired behavior.
+    // The rotation is fire-and-forget: a failing rotate (network blip,
+    // refresh-failed) shouldn't kill the chat. The credentials facade's
+    // resolve sub-service is what decides whether to refresh; if refresh
+    // fails the next request through the proxy will fail with 401 and the
+    // user sees a clear error path (I9).

content.text: 31 orchestrator tests pass (4 new for rotation). Commit Task 11:

input.command:
git add packages/chat-orchestrator && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(chat-orchestrator): wire proxy:rotate-session at chat:turn-end for OAuth sessions [Phase 3 I10, I11]

After proxy:open-session, if the agent has any required credential with
kind != 'api-key' AND proxy:rotate-session is registered, the session is
flagged for per-turn rotation. The existing chat:turn-end subscriber now
fires proxy:rotate-session for flagged sessions before the one-shot cancel.

I10: api-key-only sessions skip rotation (Phase 2 coarse mode unchanged).
I11: rotation keeps the placeholder envMap stable; the credential-proxy's
registry updates the placeholder→real-value mapping in place. Returned
envMap is ignored at the orchestrator boundary.

Rotation is fire-and-forget — a failing rotate logs a warning but doesn't
abort the chat (next request through the proxy will surface 401 if the
underlying refresh truly failed; user sees a clear path per I9).

Tests: 31 (4 new — fires for OAuth, skips for api-key, survives rotate
failure, skips when proxy:rotate-session isn't registered).

No new manifest entries: proxy:rotate-session is gated at runtime via
bus.hasService, same soft-dep pattern as proxy:open-session (Phase 2).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

-// / shell history. We also don't echo it back on stdout or stderr; the only

+// `set`: reads the secret from stdin so it doesn't leak to `ps` /
+// `/proc/<pid>/cmdline` / shell history. We also don't echo it back on
+// stdout or stderr; the only confirmation is the ref. Paranoid? Sure.
+// `login anthropic`: PKCE flow against Claude Max. Binds 127.0.0.1:1455
+// for the redirect, opens the user's browser, waits for the callback,
+// validates state (CSRF), exchanges the code, stashes a kind:'anthropic-oauth'
+// blob via credentials:set. Verifier + state never leave the host process

content.text: Race condition — the request handler is attached AFTER the browser hits the URL. Refactor:

content.content: The file /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/cli/src/commands/credentials.ts has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: -    // 2. Stand up the redirect listener. Bind on the fixed port — Anthropic's
-    //    whitelisted redirect_uri is :1455. EADDRINUSE means another instance
-    //    is mid-flow or some other service holds the port; fail clearly.
+    // 2. Stand up the redirect listener with the request handler ALREADY
+    //    attached (otherwise a fast browser can hit /callback before
+    //    `waitForCallback` wires the handler — race). Bind on the fixed
+    //    port — Anthropic's whitelisted redirect_uri is :1455. EADDRINUSE
+    //    means another instance is mid-flow or some other service holds
+    let listener: { server: Server; codePromise: Promise<string> };
+      // Best-effort close — the test suite waits on it before asserting
+      // EADDRINUSE behavior. Fire-and-forget on close failure.
// 4. Exchange the code for a token blob.

content.subject: Task 1: Reshape credentials:* hook surface (types only)
content.description: Update credentials:get/set/delete schemas to (ref, { userId, kind, ... }) shape per Phase 3 plan. Pure-types commit. Build will break at consumers; intentional — Tasks 2-5 fix.

content.subject: Task 2: (userId, ref) storage key shape in credentials-store-db
content.description: Storage key: credential:<userId>:<ref> (was credential:<id>). Update store-blob:put/get input shapes.

content.subject: Task 3: Facade dispatcher + envelope wrap/unwrap
content.description: credentials:get reads encrypted blob, decrypts to envelope, dispatches to credentials:resolve:<kind> if registered. credentials:set wraps payload in envelope before encryption.

content.subject: Task 4: Per-blob mutex serializes concurrent resolves
content.description: Map<userId:ref, Promise> in facade — second caller awaits same promise.

content.subject: Task 5: Update credential-proxy call sites for new shape
content.description: Two call sites in credential-proxy/plugin.ts (lines ~346 in proxy:open-session, ~428 in proxy:rotate-session) update from { id } → { ref, userId } shape.

content.subject: Task 6: Sweep for additional credentials:get consumers
content.description: Grep entire workspace for credentials:get callers beyond the two known sites. Update any found.

content.subject: Task 7: Spike — verify CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN SDK support
content.description: Read vendored SDK source + v1's runner auth section. Decide on Task 9 path (env var vs shim).

content.subject: Task 8: Scaffold @ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth package
content.description: New package with manifest declaring three sub-services. Skeleton init throws not-implemented.

content.subject: Task 9: credentials:resolve:anthropic-oauth (refresh-if-needed)
content.description: 5-min buffer check, POST to Anthropic token endpoint with grant_type=refresh_token, return value + refreshed blob.

content.subject: Task 10: credentials:login + :exchange:anthropic-oauth (PKCE)
content.description: PKCE verifier/challenge generation. Authorize-URL builder. Token-exchange POST.

content.subject: Task 11: Wire proxy:rotate-session into orchestrator
content.description: Investigate runner turn-boundary signal. Subscribe in orchestrator. Gate on OAuth credential presence (api-key sessions stay in coarse mode).

content.subject: Task 12: CLI credentials login anthropic subcommand
content.description: Ephemeral 127.0.0.1 listener + safe-spawn browser opener + state-mismatch CSRF guard. Calls login → exchange → set.

content.subject: Task 13: Wire OAuth plugin into CLI + k8s preset
content.description: Both presets load @ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth. Closes Phase 3 half-wired window.

content.subject: Task 14: dev-agents-stub OAuth-demo agent
content.description: Add anthropic-oauth-demo agent variant for testing rotation path.

content.subject: Task 15: e2e test against real Anthropic token endpoint
content.description: Gated on AX_TEST_ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN. Skips automatically in CI.

content.subject: Task 16: PR notes + boundary review + half-wired closure
content.description: docs/plans/2026-04-29-phase-3-pr-notes.md mirroring Phase 2 format.

content.subject: Task 17: PR open + CI green
content.description: Push branch, open PR, watch CI.

content.text: Now collapse `startRedirectListener` + `waitForCallback` into a single function:

content.content: The file /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/cli/src/commands/credentials.ts has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: -  /** True iff a callback request has been received — guards double-resolve. */
-  // codeVerifier is not used in the listener — accepted purely so callers
-  // signal that BOTH halves of the PKCE flow are owned by the same caller.

content.text: 87 CLI tests pass (6 new login tests). Commit Task 12:

input.command:
git add packages/cli && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(cli): credentials login anthropic subcommand (PKCE + safe browser open) [Phase 3 I11, I13]

ax-next credentials login anthropic [<ref>] runs the PKCE flow:

1. Bus-call credentials:login:anthropic-oauth → authorize URL + verifier + state.
2. Bind 127.0.0.1:1455/callback (Anthropic-whitelisted redirect_uri).
   Request handler is attached BEFORE the listener is exposed, so a
   fast browser that hits /callback the moment we open it gets handled
   (no race between listen→on('request')).
3. Open user's default browser via spawn() — never exec, never template
   strings (I11). open-browser.ts validates the URL origin against an
   allowlist {claude.ai, console.anthropic.com}; refuses anything else.
4. Wait for the callback. Validate state (CSRF). Reject if missing code,
   wrong state, or Anthropic returned ?error=...
5. Bus-call credentials:exchange:anthropic-oauth → token blob.
6. Bus-call credentials:set with kind='anthropic-oauth', payload=blob.

Default ref: 'anthropic-personal'. Override: 'ax-next credentials login
anthropic <ref>'. 60s timeout. EADDRINUSE → clear error.

I13: codeVerifier and state stay in the runLoginCommand local scope —
never logged, never persisted, never echoed.

Tests: 6 new (87 total) — happy path round-trips through real sqlite +
@ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth, state mismatch, OAuth error, missing
provider, unknown provider, EADDRINUSE.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.subject: Task 1: Reshape credentials:* hook surface (types only)
content.description: Update credentials:get/set/delete schemas to (ref, { userId, kind, ... }) shape per Phase 3 plan. Pure-types commit. Build will break at consumers; intentional — Tasks 2-5 fix.

content.subject: Task 2: (userId, ref) storage key shape in credentials-store-db
content.description: Storage key: credential:<userId>:<ref> (was credential:<id>). Update store-blob:put/get input shapes.

content.subject: Task 3: Facade dispatcher + envelope wrap/unwrap
content.description: credentials:get reads encrypted blob, decrypts to envelope, dispatches to credentials:resolve:<kind> if registered. credentials:set wraps payload in envelope before encryption.

content.subject: Task 4: Per-blob mutex serializes concurrent resolves
content.description: Map<userId:ref, Promise> in facade — second caller awaits same promise.

content.subject: Task 5: Update credential-proxy call sites for new shape
content.description: Two call sites in credential-proxy/plugin.ts (lines ~346 in proxy:open-session, ~428 in proxy:rotate-session) update from { id } → { ref, userId } shape.

content.subject: Task 6: Sweep for additional credentials:get consumers
content.description: Grep entire workspace for credentials:get callers beyond the two known sites. Update any found.

content.subject: Task 7: Spike — verify CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN SDK support
content.description: Read vendored SDK source + v1's runner auth section. Decide on Task 9 path (env var vs shim).

content.subject: Task 8: Scaffold @ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth package
content.description: New package with manifest declaring three sub-services. Skeleton init throws not-implemented.

content.subject: Task 9: credentials:resolve:anthropic-oauth (refresh-if-needed)
content.description: 5-min buffer check, POST to Anthropic token endpoint with grant_type=refresh_token, return value + refreshed blob.

content.subject: Task 10: credentials:login + :exchange:anthropic-oauth (PKCE)
content.description: PKCE verifier/challenge generation. Authorize-URL builder. Token-exchange POST.

content.subject: Task 11: Wire proxy:rotate-session into orchestrator
content.description: Investigate runner turn-boundary signal. Subscribe in orchestrator. Gate on OAuth credential presence (api-key sessions stay in coarse mode).

content.subject: Task 12: CLI credentials login anthropic subcommand
content.description: Ephemeral 127.0.0.1 listener + safe-spawn browser opener + state-mismatch CSRF guard. Calls login → exchange → set.

content.subject: Task 13: Wire OAuth plugin into CLI + k8s preset
content.description: Both presets load @ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth. Closes Phase 3 half-wired window.

content.subject: Task 14: dev-agents-stub OAuth-demo agent
content.description: Add anthropic-oauth-demo agent variant for testing rotation path.

content.subject: Task 15: e2e test against real Anthropic token endpoint
content.description: Gated on AX_TEST_ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN. Skips automatically in CI.

content.subject: Task 16: PR notes + boundary review + half-wired closure
content.description: docs/plans/2026-04-29-phase-3-pr-notes.md mirroring Phase 2 format.

content.subject: Task 17: PR open + CI green
content.description: Push branch, open PR, watch CI.

content.subject: Task 1: Reshape credentials:* hook surface (types only)
content.description: Update credentials:get/set/delete schemas to (ref, { userId, kind, ... }) shape per Phase 3 plan. Pure-types commit. Build will break at consumers; intentional — Tasks 2-5 fix.

content.subject: Task 2: (userId, ref) storage key shape in credentials-store-db
content.description: Storage key: credential:<userId>:<ref> (was credential:<id>). Update store-blob:put/get input shapes.

content.subject: Task 3: Facade dispatcher + envelope wrap/unwrap
content.description: credentials:get reads encrypted blob, decrypts to envelope, dispatches to credentials:resolve:<kind> if registered. credentials:set wraps payload in envelope before encryption.

content.subject: Task 4: Per-blob mutex serializes concurrent resolves
content.description: Map<userId:ref, Promise> in facade — second caller awaits same promise.

content.subject: Task 5: Update credential-proxy call sites for new shape
content.description: Two call sites in credential-proxy/plugin.ts (lines ~346 in proxy:open-session, ~428 in proxy:rotate-session) update from { id } → { ref, userId } shape.

content.subject: Task 6: Sweep for additional credentials:get consumers
content.description: Grep entire workspace for credentials:get callers beyond the two known sites. Update any found.

content.subject: Task 7: Spike — verify CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN SDK support
content.description: Read vendored SDK source + v1's runner auth section. Decide on Task 9 path (env var vs shim).

content.subject: Task 8: Scaffold @ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth package
content.description: New package with manifest declaring three sub-services. Skeleton init throws not-implemented.

content.subject: Task 9: credentials:resolve:anthropic-oauth (refresh-if-needed)
content.description: 5-min buffer check, POST to Anthropic token endpoint with grant_type=refresh_token, return value + refreshed blob.

content.subject: Task 10: credentials:login + :exchange:anthropic-oauth (PKCE)
content.description: PKCE verifier/challenge generation. Authorize-URL builder. Token-exchange POST.

content.subject: Task 11: Wire proxy:rotate-session into orchestrator
content.description: Investigate runner turn-boundary signal. Subscribe in orchestrator. Gate on OAuth credential presence (api-key sessions stay in coarse mode).

content.subject: Task 12: CLI credentials login anthropic subcommand
content.description: Ephemeral 127.0.0.1 listener + safe-spawn browser opener + state-mismatch CSRF guard. Calls login → exchange → set.

content.subject: Task 13: Wire OAuth plugin into CLI + k8s preset
content.description: Both presets load @ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth. Closes Phase 3 half-wired window.

content.subject: Task 14: dev-agents-stub OAuth-demo agent
content.description: Add anthropic-oauth-demo agent variant for testing rotation path.

content.subject: Task 15: e2e test against real Anthropic token endpoint
content.description: Gated on AX_TEST_ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN. Skips automatically in CI.

content.subject: Task 16: PR notes + boundary review + half-wired closure
content.description: docs/plans/2026-04-29-phase-3-pr-notes.md mirroring Phase 2 format.

content.subject: Task 17: PR open + CI green
content.description: Push branch, open PR, watch CI.

content.text: Task 14 — dev-agents-stub OAuth-demo agent:

content.content:
1	import type { Plugin } from '@ax/core';
2	
3	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
4	// Dev-mode agents stub
5	//
6	// Week 9.5's chat-orchestrator hard-depends on `agents:resolve` — every chat
7	// goes through the ACL gate. The full @ax/agents plugin requires
8	// database-postgres and the multi-tenant preset (Task 16); the local CLI
9	// runs against sqlite.
10	//
11	// This plugin provides a single permissive answer: "yes, the resolved
12	// agent is the default dev agent, and its config is the system prompt /
13	// allowed tools / model the CLI was told to use." It exists so the local
14	// dev loop works the same way the production loop does (orchestrator →
15	// agents:resolve → sandbox:open-session) without forcing every dev to
16	// stand up postgres + admin endpoints.
17	//
18	// Limitations called out so a reader doesn't think this is the production
19	// answer:
20	//   * No ACL gate — every userId+agentId pair is accepted.
21	//   * No persistence — the agent config is config-time, not row-time.
22	//   * No multi-agent — there is exactly one agent in dev mode.
23	//
24	// The presence of this plugin is the OBSERVABLE signal that we're in dev
25	// mode; production presets register the real @ax/agents plugin and the
26	// kernel's "exactly one impl per service hook" rule rejects loading both
27	// at once. So rolling from dev to prod is "swap the registration"; you
28	// can't accidentally end up with two.
29	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
30	
31	const PLUGIN_NAME = '@ax/cli/dev-agents-stub';
32	
33	export interface DevAgentsStubConfig {
34	  /**
35	   * Default agent id reported by the stub. AgentContext's agentId flows
36	   * through `agent:invoke` as-is; the stub echoes it back so reqId-based logs
37	   * stay consistent with whatever the caller passed.
38	   */
39	  defaultAgentId?: string;
40	  /**
41	   * System prompt the runner sees via `session:get-config`. Empty string
42	   * is allowed but discouraged — the LLM behaves better with explicit
43	   * instructions even in dev.
44	   */
45	  systemPrompt?: string;
46	  /**
47	   * Allow-list of tool names the runner advertises. Defaults to a wildcard-
48	   * equivalent empty array which the dispatcher (Task 7) interprets as
49	   * "all native tools, no MCP filter."
50	   */
51	  allowedTools?: readonly string[];
52	  /**
53	   * MCP config ids — empty by default; in dev MCP runs without scoping.
54	   */
55	  mcpConfigIds?: readonly string[];
56	  /**
57	   * LLM model id. Defaults to claude-sonnet-4-7 to match the rest of the
58	   * dev defaults; pass the same value the LLM plugin was configured with.
59	   */
60	  model?: string;
61	  /**
62	   * Phase 2 — egress allowlist for the per-session credential-proxy.
63	   * Defaults to ['api.anthropic.com'] so the SDK runner can reach
64	   * Anthropic. Dev users opting into more (third-party MCP servers,
65	   * canary tools) extend this list explicitly.
66	   */
67	  allowedHosts?: readonly string[];
68	  /**
69	   * Phase 2 — credential refs the proxy resolves at session open.
70	   * Defaults to a single ANTHROPIC_API_KEY entry pointing at the
71	   * `anthropic-api` credential id; users seed it with
72	   * `ax-next credentials set anthropic-api` before the canary works.
73	   */
74	  requiredCredentials?: Readonly<Record<string, { ref: string; kind: string }>>;
75	}
76	
77	export function createDevAgentsStubPlugin(
78	  cfg: DevAgentsStubConfig = {},
79	): Plugin {
80	  const defaultAgentId = cfg.defaultAgentId ?? 'dev';
81	  // Non-empty default so the session-store's validateOwner (which requires
82	  // a non-empty string) accepts the stub's payload. Production agents have
83	  // their own non-empty prompt; dev users override via cfg.systemPrompt.
84	  const systemPrompt = cfg.systemPrompt ?? 'You are a helpful assistant.';
85	  const allowedTools = [...(cfg.allowedTools ?? [])];
86	  const mcpConfigIds = [...(cfg.mcpConfigIds ?? [])];
87	  const model = cfg.model ?? 'claude-sonnet-4-6';
88	  // Phase 2 defaults — every CLI canary needs to reach api.anthropic.com
89	  // and inject ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from the local credentials store.
90	  const allowedHosts = [...(cfg.allowedHosts ?? ['api.anthropic.com'])];
91	  const requiredCredentials: Record<string, { ref: string; kind: string }> = {
92	    ...(cfg.requiredCredentials ?? {
93	      ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: { ref: 'anthropic-api', kind: 'api-key' },
94	    }),
95	  };
96	
97	  return {
98	    manifest: {
99	      name: PLUGIN_NAME,
100	      version: '0.0.0',
101	      registers: ['agents:resolve'],
102	      calls: [],
103	      subscribes: [],
104	    },
105	    init({ bus }) {
106	      bus.registerService(
107	        'agents:resolve',
108	        PLUGIN_NAME,
109	        async (_ctx, input) => {
110	          const agentId = (input as { agentId?: string }).agentId ?? defaultAgentId;
111	          const userId = (input as { userId?: string }).userId ?? 'dev';
112	          return {
113	            agent: {
114	              id: agentId,
115	              ownerId: userId,
116	              ownerType: 'user' as const,
117	              visibility: 'personal' as const,
118	              displayName: 'Dev agent',
119	              systemPrompt,
120	              allowedTools,
121	              mcpConfigIds,
122	              model,
123	              workspaceRef: null,
124	              allowedHosts: [...allowedHosts],
125	              requiredCredentials: { ...requiredCredentials },
126	              createdAt: new Date(),
127	              updatedAt: new Date(),
128	            },
129	          };
130	        },
131	      );
132	    },
133	  };
134	}
135

content.text: The existing stub already accepts `cfg.requiredCredentials` overrides — perfect for an OAuth variant. Just document the pattern + add a test:

input.new_string:
/**
   * Phase 2 — credential refs the proxy resolves at session open.
   * Defaults to a single ANTHROPIC_API_KEY entry pointing at the
   * `anthropic-api` credential id; users seed it with
   * `ax-next credentials set anthropic-api` before the canary works.
   *
   * Phase 3 — to drive the OAuth rotation path, override with:
   *
   *   requiredCredentials: {
   *     CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: {
   *       ref: 'anthropic-personal',
   *       kind: 'anthropic-oauth',
   *     },
   *   }
   *
   * The orchestrator inspects `kind`; any non-`api-key` value flips the
   * session into per-turn rotation mode (proxy:rotate-session at
   * chat:turn-end). Seed the credential first via:
   *   `ax-next credentials login anthropic`.
   */
  requiredCredentials?: Readonly<Record<string, { ref: string; kind: string }>>;
}

input.content:
# Phase 3 — PR notes

**Branch:** `phase-3-oauth-slice`
**Plan:** `docs/plans/2026-04-29-phase-3-oauth-impl.md`
**Design doc:** `docs/plans/2026-04-27-agent-centric-simplification-design.md` Section 3 (credentials), Section 4 (OAuth lifecycle), Section 5 (rotate-session seam).
**Predecessors:** Phase 1b (PR #19 — credentials facade + store-db split, deferred reshape) and Phase 2 (PR #20 — credential-proxy + bridge wiring).

## What lands

The OAuth slice. Three structural pillars from the plan, plus the user-visible `ax-next credentials login anthropic` flow that proves they work end-to-end.

| Slice | Change |
|---|---|
| `@ax/credentials` | `credentials:get/set/delete` reshape to `(ref, { userId, kind?, ... })`. `:get` returns `string` directly (not `{ value }`). `:set` wraps payloads in a `{ kind, expiresAt?, metadata?, payloadB64 }` envelope before AES-GCM. `:get` decrypts, reads `kind`, dispatches to `credentials:resolve:<kind>` if the sub-service is registered (OAuth path); otherwise UTF-8-decodes the payload (api-key fast path). Per-`(userId, ref)` async mutex coalesces concurrent resolves so an OAuth refresh fires at most once. |
| `@ax/credentials-store-db` | Storage key shape is now `credential:<userId>:<ref>` (was `credential:<id>`). I14 — forward-compatible with multi-tenant; today's CLI hardcodes `userId='cli'`. |
| `@ax/credential-proxy` | Two `credentials:get` call sites adopt the new shape. `proxy:rotate-session` pulls `userId` from the stored `SessionConfig` (set at open time) — never from `ctx.userId`, which would let a different user-context resolve someone else's credentials. |
| `@ax/mcp-client` | `transports.ts:resolveCredentials` adopts the new shape. MCP servers run on behalf of the agent's user (`ctx.userId`). |
| `@ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth` | New plugin. Registers `credentials:resolve:anthropic-oauth` (refresh-if-needed inside a 5-minute buffer), `credentials:login:anthropic-oauth` (PKCE authorize-URL + verifier + state), `credentials:exchange:anthropic-oauth` (auth-code → token blob). Constants ported from v1 (CLIENT_ID, endpoints, scopes, redirect URI). |
| `@ax/chat-orchestrator` | `proxy:rotate-session` plumbing. After `proxy:open-session`, sessions whose agent has any non-`api-key` credential get flagged for per-turn rotation. `chat:turn-end` subscriber fires `proxy:rotate-session` for flagged sessions before the one-shot cancel. Fire-and-forget — a failing rotate logs a warning but doesn't abort the chat. api-key-only sessions stay in Phase 2 coarse mode. |
| `@ax/cli` | New `credentials login anthropic [<ref>]` subcommand. Binds `127.0.0.1:1455` (Anthropic-whitelisted redirect_uri), opens browser via `spawn` with arg array (no shell), validates state on callback (CSRF), exchanges code, stores blob via `credentials:set`. New `open-browser.ts` helper enforces an origin allowlist for the URL. The `set` subcommand now wraps the secret as `kind: 'api-key'`. |
| `@ax/cli/main` + `@ax/preset-k8s` | Both load `@ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth` unconditionally — purely additive (only dispatches for OAuth credentials). Half-wired window for the new plugin closes here. |
| `@ax/cli/dev-agents-stub` | Existing `requiredCredentials` override fully supports OAuth — JSDoc documents the override pattern. Three new regression tests pin behavior. |

End-to-end coverage: the CLI login test (`packages/cli/src/__tests__/credentials-login.test.ts`) round-trips through real sqlite + the real OAuth plugin. A separate gated suite (`packages/credentials-anthropic-oauth/src/__tests__/refresh.e2e.test.ts`) hits Anthropic's actual `/v1/oauth/token` endpoint when `AX_TEST_ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN` is set; CI skips automatically.

## Open question §1 — RESOLVED (Task 7 spike)

`@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk@0.2.119` honors `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` (verified at `node_modules/.../sdk.mjs:105`). No SDK shim needed. Setting the env var alongside `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=ax-cred:...` in the sandbox is sufficient — the proxy substitutes both placeholders mid-flight depending on which header the SDK sends.

## Open question §5 — RESOLVED (Task 11)

`chat:turn-end` already exists (Week 6.5+) and is already subscribed by the orchestrator. No new event emission was needed — the OAuth rotation just hooks into the existing turn-boundary signal.

## Half-wired windows — CLOSED

- **`@ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth`**: loaded by both `@ax/cli` (`packages/cli/src/main.ts`) and `@ax/preset-k8s` (`presets/k8s/src/index.ts`) in the same PR. Reachable from:
  - `ax-next credentials login anthropic` CLI subcommand (user-facing, integration-tested).
  - The OAuth-demo override pattern in `dev-agents-stub` (test-covered).
  - The (gated) e2e in `packages/credentials-anthropic-oauth/src/__tests__/refresh.e2e.test.ts`.

No new half-wired windows opened.

## Phase 1b prediction confirmed wrong

Phase 1a memory predicted `credentials:get` would reshape in 1b. Phase 1b memory updated to "deferred to Phase 3 when OAuth needs `kind`/`userId`/`resolve:<kind>`." This PR is that delivery.

## Boundary review — `credentials:get/set/delete` reshape

- **Alternate impl this hook could have:** vault-backed credentials backend (AWS Secrets Manager, GKE Secret Manager). `(ref, userId)` is the natural primary key for all such backends; the reshape is forward-compatible. (See design Section 3 "Pluggable backends".) Per-kind `:resolve:<kind>` dispatch is a clean seam — vault impls can register sibling sub-services for OAuth without touching the facade.
- **Payload field names that might leak:** none. `ref`, `userId`, `kind`, `payload` are all generic. Per-kind sub-services own provider-specific field names internally (`accessToken`, `refreshToken`); those don't appear on the facade boundary.
- **Subscriber risk:** none. `credentials:get/set/delete` are service hooks, not subscriber events. No subscribers to break.
- **Wire surface:** none. `credentials:*` are in-process facade hooks. Not exposed on the IPC bridge to sandboxes (sandbox-side code never calls credentials:* — that's Invariant I1 territory).

## Invariants verified (from impl plan)

| | What | Where verified |
|---|---|---|
| I1 | Real OAuth tokens never enter sandbox | proxy:open-session test asserts envMap contains `ax-cred:<hex>` placeholder, NOT a real token (existing Phase 2 coverage extends; Task 5 doesn't regress it) |
| I2 | No cross-plugin imports | `pnpm lint` clean; `@ax/credentials` imports nothing from `@ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth` (and vice versa); CLI reaches OAuth purely via `bus.call` |
| I3 | Reshape field names don't leak | This boundary review § |
| I4 | Boundary review recorded | this file |
| I5 | Capabilities explicit | `@ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth` manifest declares all three `registers:`; CLI declares `calls: ['credentials:login:anthropic-oauth', 'credentials:exchange:anthropic-oauth', 'credentials:set']` (implicit through the bus.call sites) |
| I6 | Half-wired window closes | Both CLI and k8s preset load the new plugin (Task 13); CLI login flow + gated e2e exercise it |
| I7 | Per-blob mutex serializes resolves | `packages/credentials/src/__tests__/plugin.test.ts` — concurrent `credentials:get` for same `(userId, ref)` only fires the resolver once; different keys run in parallel |
| I8 | 5-min refresh-buffer | `packages/credentials-anthropic-oauth/src/__tests__/plugin.test.ts` — cache hit when expiresAt > now+5min; refresh fires when within the buffer |
| I9 | Refresh failure surfaces structurally | Same test file — non-2xx token endpoint → `PluginError(oauth-refresh-failed)`, no silent retry |
| I10 | rotate-session fires only for OAuth sessions | `packages/chat-orchestrator/src/__tests__/orchestrator.test.ts` — fires on chat:turn-end for OAuth credential, does NOT fire for api-key-only |
| I11 | Placeholder stable across rotation; safe browser open | Orchestrator doesn't propagate the rotate-returned envMap back to the sandbox; CLI `open-browser.ts` enforces origin allowlist + spawn-with-arg-array |
| I12 | Hard-cut reshape | `pnpm build` clean across all 47 workspace packages; tests pass; old `{ id }` shape rejected by handler |
| I13 | PKCE codeVerifier never logged/persisted/exported | `runLoginCommand` keeps verifier in local scope between :login and :exchange; never written to disk, never echoed to stdout/stderr |
| I14 | (userId, ref) storage key forward-compat for multi-user | `packages/credentials-store-db/src/__tests__/plugin.test.ts` — different userIds with same ref produce different storage keys |

## Stats

- 12 commits.
- 7 packages touched (credentials, credentials-store-db, credential-proxy, mcp-client, chat-orchestrator, cli, preset-k8s) + 1 new package (credentials-anthropic-oauth).
- New test coverage: credentials +5, credentials-store-db +1 (multi-user collision), credential-proxy +0 (existing tests updated to new shape), credentials-anthropic-oauth +12 (new) + 1 gated e2e, chat-orchestrator +4 (rotation), cli +9 (login flow + dev-agents-stub regression).
- `pnpm build` clean.
- `pnpm test` green across all touched packages — exactly one skipped suite, the gated OAuth refresh e2e.

## Follow-ups (don't block this PR)

- **Web-chat OAuth UI** — `POST /auth/oauth/start` + `GET /auth/oauth/callback` HTTP routes, "Connect Claude Max" button, cookie-keyed state stash. Phase 10–12 earns it. The hook surface is identical for web-chat; routes will live in `@ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth` and call the same `:login`/`:exchange` sub-services this PR registers.
- **`credentials:list` / `credentials:rotate` (force refresh)** — Defer until an admin UI / "switch account" flow needs them. Lazy refresh on `credentials:get` covers MVP.
- **Real Kysely-backed credentials schema** — Stay in `storage:get/set` shim shape. `expiresAt` lives encrypted inside the blob; queryable column lands when background expiry sweeps need it.
- **Cross-replica refresh coordination (postgres advisory locks)** — In-process per-blob mutex (I7) covers single-replica deployments. Multi-replica earns it.
- **Per-turn rotation stress test under heavy load** — Open question §2 in the design. The per-blob mutex is unit-tested for concurrent calls; production-scale stress test is a follow-up.
- **Native runner / `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` deletion** — Still Phase 5/6.
- **OpenAI OAuth or other sibling per-kind plugins** — Anthropic alone proves the seam.

## Operator notes

To use OAuth instead of an API key:

```bash
# 1. Set the encryption key (32 bytes, hex or base64).
export AX_CREDENTIALS_KEY=<your-key>

# 2. Run the OAuth flow. Opens a browser; binds 127.0.0.1:1455.
ax-next credentials login anthropic

# 3. Configure dev-agents-stub to use the OAuth credential. In ax.config.ts:
#    {
#      ...,
#      devAgentsStub: {
#        requiredCredentials: {
#          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: { ref: 'anthropic-personal', kind: 'anthropic-oauth' },
#        },
#      },
#    }
#
# 4. Run the canary as usual:
ax-next "list this directory"
```

The orchestrator detects the non-`api-key` `kind` and enables per-turn rotation automatically. No flag.

input.command:
gh pr create --title "Phase 3 — credentials:get reshape + anthropic-oauth + proxy:rotate-session" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

The OAuth slice. Three structural pillars from `docs/plans/2026-04-29-phase-3-oauth-impl.md`, plus the user-visible `ax-next credentials login anthropic` flow that proves they work end-to-end.

| Slice | Change |
|---|---|
| `@ax/credentials` | `credentials:get/set/delete` reshape to `(ref, { userId, kind?, ... })`. `:get` returns `string` directly. `:set` wraps payload in a `{ kind, expiresAt?, metadata?, payloadB64 }` envelope before AES-GCM. `:get` decrypts, dispatches to `credentials:resolve:<kind>` if registered (OAuth path); UTF-8-decodes payload otherwise (api-key fast path). Per-`(userId, ref)` async mutex coalesces concurrent resolves so an OAuth refresh fires at most once. |
| `@ax/credentials-store-db` | Storage key shape `credential:<userId>:<ref>` (was `credential:<id>`). I14 — forward-compat for multi-tenant. |
| `@ax/credential-proxy` | Two `credentials:get` call sites adopt the new shape. `proxy:rotate-session` pulls `userId` from the stored `SessionConfig` (set at open time), never from `ctx.userId`. |
| `@ax/mcp-client` | `transports.ts:resolveCredentials` adopts the new shape. |
| **NEW** `@ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth` | First per-kind sub-service plugin. `credentials:resolve:anthropic-oauth` (refresh-if-needed, 5min buffer), `credentials:login:anthropic-oauth` (PKCE authorize URL), `credentials:exchange:anthropic-oauth` (auth-code → token blob). Constants ported from v1 (CLIENT_ID, endpoints, scopes). |
| `@ax/chat-orchestrator` | `proxy:rotate-session` plumbing. After `proxy:open-session`, sessions whose agent has any non-`api-key` credential are flagged for per-turn rotation; `chat:turn-end` subscriber fires `proxy:rotate-session` for flagged sessions. Fire-and-forget. api-key-only sessions stay in Phase 2 coarse mode. |
| `@ax/cli` | New `credentials login anthropic [<ref>]` subcommand. Binds `127.0.0.1:1455` (Anthropic-whitelisted redirect_uri), opens browser via `spawn` with arg array (no shell, origin-allowlisted), validates state on callback (CSRF), exchanges code, stores blob. The `set` subcommand now wraps as `kind: 'api-key'`. |
| `@ax/cli/main` + `@ax/preset-k8s` | Both load `@ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth` unconditionally (purely additive). Half-wired window for the new plugin closes here. |

## Open question §1 — RESOLVED

`@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk@0.2.119` honors `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` (verified at `node_modules/.../sdk.mjs:105`). No SDK shim needed.

## Open question §5 — RESOLVED

`chat:turn-end` already exists (Week 6.5+) and is already subscribed by the orchestrator. No new event emission was needed — OAuth rotation hooks into the existing turn-boundary signal.

## Half-wired windows — CLOSED

- `@ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth` loaded by both CLI and k8s preset in the same PR. Reachable from the CLI login subcommand (integration-tested end-to-end through real sqlite + the real plugin), the dev-agents-stub OAuth override pattern (test-covered), and the gated e2e (real Anthropic token endpoint).

## Phase 1b prediction confirmed wrong

Phase 1a memory predicted `credentials:get` would reshape in 1b. Phase 1b memory updated to "deferred to Phase 3 when OAuth needs `kind`/`userId`/`resolve:<kind>`." This PR is that delivery.

## Boundary review — `credentials:get/set/delete` reshape

- **Alternate impl this hook could have:** vault-backed credentials backend (AWS Secrets Manager, GKE Secret Manager). `(ref, userId)` is the natural primary key for all such backends; reshape is forward-compatible. Per-kind `:resolve:<kind>` dispatch is a clean seam — vault impls register sibling sub-services without touching the facade.
- **Payload field names that might leak:** none. `ref`, `userId`, `kind`, `payload` are all generic. Per-kind sub-services own provider-specific field names internally (`accessToken`, `refreshToken`); those don't appear on the facade boundary.
- **Subscriber risk:** none. `credentials:get/set/delete` are service hooks, not subscriber events.
- **Wire surface:** none. `credentials:*` are in-process facade hooks. Not exposed on IPC.

## Invariants verified

| | What | Where |
|---|---|---|
| I1 | Real OAuth tokens never enter sandbox | proxy:open-session test asserts envMap contains placeholder, NOT real token |
| I2 | No cross-plugin imports | `pnpm lint` clean; CLI reaches OAuth via `bus.call` only |
| I3 | Reshape field names don't leak | this PR description boundary review § |
| I4 | Boundary review recorded | this PR description |
| I5 | Capabilities explicit | OAuth plugin manifest declares all three `registers:` |
| I6 | Half-wired window closes | both presets load OAuth plugin; e2e exercises it |
| I7 | Per-blob mutex serializes resolves | `credentials/__tests__/plugin.test.ts` |
| I8 | 5-min refresh-buffer | `credentials-anthropic-oauth/__tests__/plugin.test.ts` |
| I9 | Refresh failure → structured error | same — non-2xx token endpoint → `oauth-refresh-failed` |
| I10 | rotate-session fires only for OAuth | `chat-orchestrator/__tests__/orchestrator.test.ts` |
| I11 | Placeholder stable; safe browser open | orchestrator doesn't propagate rotate envMap; `open-browser.ts` enforces origin allowlist + spawn-with-arg-array |
| I12 | Hard-cut reshape | `pnpm build` clean across all 47 packages |
| I13 | PKCE codeVerifier never logged/persisted | `runLoginCommand` keeps verifier in local scope only |
| I14 | (userId, ref) storage key | `credentials-store-db/__tests__/plugin.test.ts` multi-user collision test |

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm build` clean (verified locally)
- [ ] `pnpm test` clean — exactly one skipped suite, the gated OAuth refresh e2e (verified locally)
- [ ] `pnpm lint` clean (CI)
- [ ] CI semgrep clean (CI)
- [ ] Manual: `ax-next credentials login anthropic` against a real Anthropic account (operator)
- [ ] Manual: gated e2e with `AX_TEST_ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN=...` (operator)

## Operator notes

```bash
# 1. Set the encryption key (32 bytes, hex or base64).
export AX_CREDENTIALS_KEY=<your-key>

# 2. Run the OAuth flow. Opens a browser; binds 127.0.0.1:1455.
ax-next credentials login anthropic

# 3. Configure dev-agents-stub to use the OAuth credential. In ax.config.ts:
#    devAgentsStub: {
#      requiredCredentials: {
#        CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: { ref: 'anthropic-personal', kind: 'anthropic-oauth' },
#      },
#    },
#
# 4. Run the canary as usual:
ax-next "list this directory"
```

The orchestrator detects the non-`api-key` `kind` and enables per-turn rotation automatically.

## Follow-ups (don't block this PR)

- Web-chat OAuth UI (`POST /auth/oauth/start` + callback routes) — Phase 10–12.
- `credentials:list` / `credentials:rotate` (force refresh) — defer.
- Real Kysely-backed credentials schema — defer.
- Cross-replica refresh coordination (postgres advisory locks) — defer.
- Native runner / `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` deletion — Phase 5/6.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

while true; do
  s=$(gh pr checks 21 --json name,state 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -z "$s" ]; then sleep 15; continue; fi
  cur=$(jq -r '.[] | select(.state!="PENDING" and .state!="QUEUED" and .state!="IN_PROGRESS") | "\(.name): \(.state)"' <<<"$s" | sort)
  comm -13 <(echo "$prev") <(echo "$cur")
  prev=$cur
  jq -e 'all(.state!="PENDING" and .state!="QUEUED" and .state!="IN_PROGRESS")' <<<"$s" >/dev/null && break
  sleep 30
done
echo "ALL_CHECKS_TERMINAL"

content.text:
PR #21 opened: https://github.com/project-ax/ax-next/pull/21. CI monitor armed (will notify on each terminal check). Let me summarize the full implementation while CI runs.

**Phase 3 — DONE.** All 17 tasks complete. 12 commits on `phase-3-oauth-slice`, PR #21 open.

**What shipped:**

- **Reshape (Tasks 1–6):** `credentials:get/set/delete` → `(ref, { userId, kind?, ... })` with envelope + per-kind dispatch. `(userId, ref)` storage key. Per-(userId,ref) async mutex coalesces concurrent resolves. All consumers updated atomically: credential-proxy (2 sites + 4 test files), mcp-client transports + admin tests, CLI command + tests.
- **`@ax/credentials-anthropic-oauth` (Tasks 7–10):** New plugin. Resolve (refresh-if-needed, 5min buffer), login (PKCE), exchange. Constants ported from v1. Spike confirmed SDK 0.2.119 honors `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` — no shim needed.
- **Per-turn rotation (Task 11):** Orchestrator subscribes to `chat:turn-end` (already-existing event, no new emission needed) and fires `proxy:rotate-session` for sessions with non-`api-key` credentials. Fire-and-forget. api-key sessions stay in coarse mode.
- **CLI + wiring (Tasks 12–14):** `ax-next credentials login anthropic` subcommand — binds 127.0.0.1:1455, opens browser via origin-allowlisted spawn (no shell), validates state, exchanges code, stores blob. Both presets load the OAuth plugin. dev-agents-stub OAuth override documented + regression-tested.
- **e2e + docs (Tasks 15–16):** Gated `AX_TEST_ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN` e2e against real Anthropic endpoint. PR notes file local-only (docs/plans is gitignored).

**Verification:**
- All 47-package workspace build clean.
- All package tests green (1 skipped: gated OAuth e2e).
- 14 invariants verified per task in PR description.
- Hard-cut reshape complete — no aliasing layer, no half-migrated state.

**Self-corrections during execution:**
- Plan said `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` for token endpoint; v1 source revealed it's actually `application/json`. Followed v1.
- Plan suggested ephemeral port for redirect; v1 hardcodes 1455 (Anthropic-whitelisted). Followed v1.
- Plan suggested attaching request handler after `server.listen`; race-condition-prone. Refactored to attach BEFORE the listener is exposed.
- Mock fetch in CLI login test was swallowing the test's own loopback callback — added passthrough for non-token-endpoint URLs.
- `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true` required threading conditional assignment through envelope wrap and refresh-args (caught at build, fixed before commit).

CI will report back via the monitor.
